channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: The Gulf of Whatnow? Mapmakers grapple with Trump's geographic renaming plans
item.description: What's in a name change, after all? The water bordered by the Southern United States, Mexico and Cuba will be critical to shipping lanes and vacationers whether it’s called the Gulf of Mexico, as it has been for four centuries, or the Gulf of America, as President Donald Trump ordered this week.
item.dc:creator: LAURIE KELLMAN and HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A rare plant emits a stink of death when it blooms. Thousands in Australia queued to get close to it
item.description: SYDNEY (AP) — The rare unfurling of an endangered plant that emits the smell of decaying flesh drew hundreds of devoted fans to a greenhouse in Sydney on Thursday where they lined up to experience a momentous bloom -– and a fragrance evoking gym socks and rotting garbage.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY and RICK RYCROFT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Firefighters battle to maintain the upper hand on a huge fire north of Los Angeles
item.description: CASTAIC, Calif. (AP) — Firefighters fought to maintain the upper hand on a huge and rapidly moving wildfire that swept through rugged mountains north of Los Angeles and resulted in more than 50,000 people being put under evacuation orders or warnings.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER and MARCIO JOSE SANCHEZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Teen fatally shoots a female student and himself at Antioch High School in Nashville, police say
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — A shooting in a Nashville high school cafeteria Wednesday left a female student dead and another student wounded, nearly two years after another deadly school shooting in the city that ignited an emotional debate about gun control in Tennessee.
item.dc:creator: KRISTIN M. HALL, TRAVIS LOLLER and JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Amanda Knox gets a final shot at clearing her name of slander in Italy's top court
item.description: ROME (AP) — Amanda Knox has a final shot at clearing her name of the last vestige of criminal wrongdoing when Italy’s highest court on Thursday hears her appeal of a slander conviction for falsely accusing a Congolese bar owner in the 2007 murder of her British flatmate.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Teen who killed 3 girls at Taylor Swift-themed dance class in England faces sentencing
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A violence-obsessed teenager is facing decades in prison when he is sentenced for stabbing three young girls to death at a Taylor Swift-themed summer dance class.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A bid to block Trump's cancellation of birthright citizenship is in federal court
item.description: A federal judge in Seattle is set to hear the first arguments Thursday in a multi-state lawsuit seeking to block President Donald Trump’s executive order ending the constitutional guarantee of birthright citizenship regardless of the parents' immigration status. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: The Oscar nominations are Thursday. Here's what to look for
item.description: In the wake of devastating wildfires in Los Angeles that struck at the heart of the movie industry, nominations to the 97th Academy Awards are going forward Thursday morning after a pair of delays.
item.dc:creator: JAKE COYLE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Over 50,000 under evacuation orders or warnings as wildfire imperils homes north of Los Angeles
item.description: CASTAIC, Calif. (AP) — More than 50,000 people were under evacuation orders or warnings Wednesday as a huge and fast-moving wildfire swept through rugged mountains north of Los Angeles, but fire officials said a rapid ground and air assault was giving them the upper hand.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER and MARCIO JOSE SANCHEZ, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Oath Keepers founder Stewart Rhodes visits Capitol Hill after Trump clemency
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Oath Keepers founder Stewart Rhodes, who was convicted of orchestrating his far-right extremist group’s Jan. 6, 2021 assault on the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Las Vegas hotel workers union reaches deal with casino to end longest strike in decades
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — The longest strike in decades by Las Vegas hotel workers ended Wednesday with a new contract between the union and a casino where hundreds of employees had walked off the job in November.
item.dc:creator: RIO YAMAT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Indiana couple abused 33 kids over 13 years: Docs
item.description: Details have been released on how 33 children were treated in the Indiana home of reported abusers Sonja and Brian Stafford for approximately 13 years.
item.dc:creator: Deja Studdard, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former Arkansas officer arrested, charged with assault in beating of handcuffed inmate
item.description: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — A former Arkansas police officer who was caught on video beating a handcuffed inmate in the back of his patrol car last year has been arrested and charged with aggravated assault.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Child dies, officer critical in local shooting
item.description: A 4-year-old child has died and a police officer is in critical condition following an officer-involved shooting in Columbiana County Wednesday
item.dc:creator: Gerry Ricciutti, NewsNation

item.title: Speaker Johnson forms committee to probe Jan. 6, Democrats' original investigation
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) on Wednesday said he will establish a new select subcommittee that will probe the Jan. 6, 2021, Capitol riot — and likely dig into the now-defunct Jan. 6 select committee that was led by Rep. Bennie Thompson (D-Miss.) and then-Rep. Liz Cheney (R-Wyo.). Rep.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Meta denies platforms are forcing users to follow Trump, Vance
item.description: Meta is pushing back on claims that Facebook and Instagram users are being forced into following accounts for President Trump and Vice President Vance.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, Fox 5

item.title: Mistrial declared after New Hampshire jury deadlocks in youth detention center rape case
item.description: CONCORD, N.H. (AP) — A judge declared a mistrial Wednesday after jurors deadlocked on whether a former worker at New Hampshire's youth detention center raped a 14-year-old boy in 1998, but the defendant still faces 15 other charges in separate cases.
item.dc:creator: HOLLY RAMER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Intoxicated mother, 2 kids found lying on freezing Texas sidewalk, sheriff's office says
item.description: A Texas mother is facing charges after she and her two young children were found lying outside as snow fell and temperatures dropped below freezing in Houston this week.
item.dc:creator: Emily Smith, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge Chutkan: Trump pardons won't change 'tragic truth' of Jan. 6 Capitol riot
item.description: U.S. District Judge Tanya Chutkan, who oversaw President Trump's federal election subversion case, wrote in court filings that his sweeping pardons for those charged in connection with the Jan. 6, 2021 Capitol attack could not change the “tragic truth” of that day.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: House passes Laken Riley Act, sending it to Trump for first legislative win
item.description: The House on Wednesday passed the Laken Riley Act, sending the immigration-related bill to President Trump’s desk in what is poised to be his first legislative victory since returning to the White House this week. The chamber cleared the bill in a 263-156 vote.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, Fox 5

item.title: Fire chief shot, killed after stopping to help driver who struck a deer in Alabama
item.description: “We lost one of our brothers, leaders, mentor, and family to a senseless tragedy,” Cauthen's colleagues wrote in a Facebook post.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth White, ABC 4

item.title: Lynn Ban, star of Netflix reality show, dies at 52 after emergency surgery
item.description: Lynn Ban, who starred in Netflix's reality show "Bling Empire: New York," has died weeks after a skiing accident and emergency brain surgery.
item.dc:creator: Emily Smith, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NYC Mayor Adams: I'm 'at a loss' on how to handle migrants in NYC
item.description: Adams criticized the Biden administration for their border policies in an interview with Tucker Carlson.
item.dc:creator: Caitlyn Becker, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s blanket Jan. 6 pardons stun Republicans on Capitol Hill
item.description: President Donald Trump’s sweeping pardons of more than 1,500 people charged with crimes related to the Capitol riot of Jan. 6, 2021, including individuals who assaulted police officers, stunned Republican lawmakers who witnessed firsthand the chaos on Capitol Hill four years ago.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: West Virginia mother on trial for locking adopted teenagers in shed denies allegations
item.description: A West Virginia mother denied forcing two of their adopted children to stay in a shed despite police locating them in the shed behind a locked door.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's FBI overhaul puts 'untouchable' feds on notice: former agent
item.description: President Trump has promised a monumental shift within the FBI with a major change in leadership at the very top after former Director Christopher Wray and acting director Paul Abbate resigned.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: As fire-fanning winds continue, Southern California readies for potential rain and toxic runoff
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Parched Southern California continued to face dangerous winds but could get some badly needed rain this weekend, forecasters said Tuesday, dampening the prospects of another round of killer wildfires though even a small amount of precipitation could could create new challenges lik
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 11-year-old pulls ultra rare Paul Skenes rookie card, Pirates says massive trade deal stands
item.description: The card, which contains the "Debut Patch" worn by Paul Skenes, quickly became a highly-coveted item. After months of search, an 11-year-old has reportedly come up with it.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, WGN 9

item.title: Trump wants to pull the US out of the World Health Organization again. Here's what may happen next
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump used one of the flurry of executive actions that he issued on his first day back in the White House to begin the process of withdrawing the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARIA CHENG and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 5 takeaways as Trump returns to power
item.description: President Trump took office again Monday, completing a remarkable political comeback and becoming only the second person to serve nonconsecutive terms as commander in chief.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, Fox 5

item.title: The baby names going 'extinct' in 2025, according to BabyCenter
item.description: Much like the doomed flightless dodo, baby names like “Emory,” “Jamie” or “Remy” are quickly being stamped out by the same humans who once found them so uniquely remarkable.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4
